meta.source: TradingView watchlist + FRED live scrape (Chrome extension) -- real-rate framework updated: DFII5/DFII10 now primary, DGS2-EXPINF2YR demoted to secondary proxy

confidence_meta.reason: No binary catalyst within 48h post-auction; MOVE (72.41) below 80 threshold; scores 3 improving / 1 stable, not split 2-2

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Policy-Tight / Real-Rate Tightening (DFII5 elevated) / Oil Shock Risk
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Semis and enabling-layer chain leading (DRAM/CRDO/ASX/GLW/LITE/AMKR/MU +5-12%); SOXX/SPY ratio up to 0.786
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 weekly Fed balance sheet release (today, Jul 9) -- watch for updated TGA/reserves
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW pass only (EWY below SMA20, KOSPI below 8,200)

bias: DFII5 (5Y TIPS real yield) is the primary real-rate signal — elevated and rising. T5YIE and T10YIE stable means inflation expectations anchored, so the yield pressure is real-rate tightening not inflation panic. Oil +5.83% today threatens the disinflation thesis that would allow DFII5 to fall. USDJPY broke 162. MOVE spiked pre-auction. Reduce high-beta until DFII5 shows sustained decline.

supporting: 10Y yield -3.6bps to 4.541% with credit spreads flat-to-tighter
Enabling-layer chain up 5-12% across the board (DRAM, CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI)
TGA drew down ~$38.8B/wk, adding reserves back to the banking system
against: MOVE still climbing, +3.07% to 72.41 -- bond vol not confirming equity calm
USDJPY still above 162 BOJ intervention threshold
DFII5 (1.99%) and DFII10 (2.30%) -- both market-based real yields -- remain historically elevated, a structural headwind for long-duration growth/semis
DFII5/DFII10 are as of Jul 7 (latest published H.15 print); today's print not yet available at scrape time
counterargs.read: Today's data points broadly toward easing conditions -- falling yields, calmer equity vol, a strong semis bid, and a pullback in oil. A data-quality fix also matters here: the front-end 'real-rate tightening pain trade' that's been the throughline of recent runs was partly an artifact of a stale placeholder breakeven. With EXPINF2YR now live, real 2Y yield reads 1.41%, well below the ~2.2%+ previously reported -- the front end looks less restrictive than believed. Bond-market vol (MOVE) is still climbing and the structural gates (Korea, BOJ threshold) haven't opened, so this isn't an all-clear, but both the price action and the corrected data argue for less alarm than recent runs implied.

trade_table.rationale: Broad 5-12% rally across DRAM, CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI; SOXX +4.9%, SOXX/SPY ratio rising to 0.786. Confirm follow-through before adding size.
10Y fell 3.6bps with breakevens stable -- consistent with an 'easing' read, not recession fear. MOVE still elevated keeps conviction capped.
HY OAS flat, HYG +0.19%; consumer-credit names up 2-5% today after a rough prior session.
Gate closed 1/3 -- EWY ~13pts below SMA20, KOSPI ~900pts below 8,200 trigger. No positioning until gate opens.
Still above 162 BOJ intervention threshold despite -0.20% pullback; headline risk remains live.

catalysts.event: H.4.1 weekly Fed balance sheet release
catalysts.note: TGA/reserves/WALCL update for week ending Jul 8

duration_score.reason: DFII5 (5Y TIPS real yield) is elevated and rising — primary real-rate pressure signal. Oil +5.83% today threatens the disinflation thesis that would allow DFII5 to fall. 10Y at 4.577%, MOVE spiking +6.82% pre-auction. DFII5 above prior highs = tightening regime risk for semis/growth.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Corrected data show real 2Y yield at 1.41%, not the ~2.2%+ implied by the prior stale placeholder -- meaningfully less restrictive at the front end than the multi-day 'pain trade' narrative suggested
james_framework.pain_trade: DFII5 rising = real-rate pressure on high-beta. Not CPI panic — TIPS market directly pricing in sustained real tightening.
james_framework.watch_signal: If DFII5 starts falling → real rate relief → semis/growth bid. Watch DFII5 daily.

dfii5.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (DFII5)
dfii5.source: TradingView DFII5 — market-based, daily, direct TIPS pricing
dfii5.note: PRIMARY real-rate signal. Market-based TIPS real yield — cleaner than modeled proxies. Rising DFII5 = pressure on growth/semis/long-duration. Falling DFII5 = relief for high-beta.

dfii10.label: 10Y TIPS real yield (DFII10)
dfii10.source: TradingView DFII10 — if available in watchlist
dfii10.note: Long-duration equity valuation pressure. Confirms DFII5 signal.

t5yie.label: 5Y breakeven inflation (T5YIE)
t5yie.note: Inflation expectations confirmation. Stable = inflation not re-accelerating despite yield move.

t10yie.label: 10Y breakeven inflation (T10YIE)
t10yie.note: Long-end inflation expectations. Stable = market not pricing structural inflation return.

real_yield_primary.label: Primary real-rate pressure signal
real_yield_primary.note: DFII5 is the headline real-rate input for DRAM/SOXX/QQQ framework. See trading interpretation table.

real_yield_10y_derived.note: Derived 10Y real yield — secondary confirmation. Use DFII5/DFII10 as primary.

two_y_proxy.label: 2Y modeled real-rate proxy
two_y_proxy.note: Experimental secondary proxy. Not central — DFII5 is the primary signal per James framework.

hierarchy.4: DGS2 − EXPINF2YR — Secondary modeled front-end proxy only

trading_interpretation.dfii5_falling: Supports high-beta growth / semis / DRAM / SOXX / QQQ
trading_interpretation.dfii5_rising_sharply: Pressure on growth, semis, long-duration assets
trading_interpretation.dfii5_above_prior_highs: Tightening regime risk — reduce high-beta exposure

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: T5YIE and T10YIE stable despite oil surge — inflation expectations anchored. DFII5 rising = real-rate tightening channel, not inflation channel.

chain.note: CLOSE PRIORITY -- still monitor $7.50/$10 Jul 17 spread

move.note: Bond vol still climbing, not confirming equity calm

sector_rotation.current_regime: Broad Risk-On Bounce / Tentative Liquidity Expansion

actionable_output.laggards: Long-duration Treasuries lag on a relative basis despite the absolute yield decline
actionable_output.note: One-day confirmation only -- needs follow-through before conviction rises

primary.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (market-based)
primary.note: Market-based, updates daily, directly reflects real yield from inflation-indexed Treasuries. Least distorted by near-term Fed policy noise of the tenors tracked here. This is now the headline real-rate signal for the dashboard, replacing the modeled 2Y proxy in that role.

confirmation.label: 10Y TIPS real yield (market-based)
confirmation.note: Long-duration equity valuation pressure gauge. Cross-checks closely against the US10Y - T10YIE breakeven calc (2.281%) -- consistent read, small gap is methodology (constant-maturity TIPS vs. breakeven).

inflation_context.note: Market breakevens for confirmation only -- not used to derive real yields directly here since DFII5/DFII10 are already market-priced real yields.

secondary_proxy.label: Modeled front-end real-rate proxy (experimental)
secondary_proxy.note: EXPINF2YR is Cleveland Fed model-based expected inflation, not a market breakeven, and there is no clean market-based 2Y TIPS benchmark (DFII2 is not a standard published series). Kept as a secondary/experimental front-end read only -- DFII5 is now the primary real-rate signal per dashboard hierarchy.

trading_read.dfii5_rising_sharply: Pressure on growth, semis, long-duration assets

real_rate_pressure.hierarchy_note: Rank 1: DFII5 (primary). Rank 2: DFII10 (confirmation). Rank 3: T5YIE/T10YIE (inflation-expectation confirmation). Rank 4: DGS2-EXPINF2YR (secondary/experimental front-end proxy only).
